steps.title: Choose the right model
steps.content: Doubling at a fixed interval is exponential growth with base 2. The standard form is $$M(t) = M_0 \cdot 2^{t/T},$$ where $M_0$ is the starting value and $T$ is the doubling period.

steps.title: Plug in the given values
steps.content: The account starts at $M_0 = 890$ dollars and doubles every $T = 10$ years, giving $$M(t) = 890 \cdot 2^{t/10}.$$ Quick check at $t=10$: $890 \cdot 2^1 = 1780$, which is double the original — correct.

steps.title: Evaluate the choices
steps.content:
• A. $M(t)=890\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^{t/10}$ — this halves every 10 years, the opposite of doubling.
  • B. $M(t)=890\left(\frac{1}{10}\right)^{t/2}$ — decays toward zero, not doubling.
  • C. $M(t)=890(2)^{t/10}$ — doubles every 10 years as required.
  • D. $M(t)=890(10)^{t/2}$ — grows tenfold every 2 years, far too fast.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
